Hit a CORS Wall and Can't Touch the Backend? Wrap It With Google Apps Script

In modern frontend dev, you reach for AJAX to grab remote data all the time. And just as often, you run face-first into CORS. So let me jot down how to use Google's Google Apps Script to get around the CORS problem.
Before we touch Google Apps Script, let's do a quick crash course on what CORS actually is.

. Normally the backend exposes an API for the frontend to hit with AJAX. But if it just opened that API up to anyone on the internet, that's a big security hole. So this ties into something called the
. Let me throw out an example to explain what that policy is.

Say you've got a fridge and a TV at home. You want to open the fridge, you open it. You want to watch TV, you turn it on. That's basically the same-origin policy — it's your house, you can do whatever you want. Flip it around, and you can't just walk into someone else's place and open their fridge, watch their TV, or take a shower.
So inside your own house, that's same-origin. The moment you mess with someone else's house, that's a different origin — that's cross-origin, and that comes with a bunch of restrictions.
Back to CORS. CORS is a browser spec. It lets us fetch data from a different domain over the web while still working around the same-origin policy problem.

The two JavaScript methods you'll usually use with CORS are XMLHttpRequest or Fetch. Some data is restricted so it can only be accessed under the same domain. So if you're on a different domain, you get a CORS error.
A lot of people think of CORS as one of those must-have frontend skills. But honestly, CORS is mostly tied to the backend, because you have to set the right header on the backend side.

Calling an API from the frontend and getting slapped with a CORS error, but you can't change the backend? This post starts from the idea of the Same-origin policy, then uses Google Apps Script to wrap a little proxy around a third-party API. That turns it into your own same-origin API and gets you past the CORS restriction.
